headline: OpenAI sunsetting fine-tuning while advancing voice AI capabilities
body: OpenAI is discontinuing its fine-tuning service while simultaneously releasing GPT-Realtime-2 with advanced reasoning capabilities for voice interactions. This shift reflects the vendor's pivot toward pre-trained models and real-time conversational AI over custom model adaptation. For healthcare AI implementations, this means organizations relying on fine-tuned models for clinical documentation or EHR integration need immediate migration strategies, while voice-enabled clinical workflows could benefit from improved reasoning in patient interaction scenarios. The deprecation signals a consolidation in OpenAI's product strategy that will force healthcare vendors and health systems to reassess their technical dependencies and consider alternative customization approaches.
